title: Speech-Language Pathologist

rationales.taskAutomation: Standard screening-assessment scoring and session documentation carry a meaningful digital share; therapy delivery and clinical judgment do not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI voice-analysis and speech-therapy apps are credible and improving, increasingly used alongside in-person and teletherapy sessions.
rationales.adoption: School systems and telehealth-enabled practices are early-to-mid adopters of AI speech-therapy tools; broader adoption is still building.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can score standardized assessments and generate home-practice materials, but therapy delivery stays with the clinician.

tasksGrowing: Direct therapy delivery and technique adjustment
Caregiver training and coordination with other providers
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1127 (Speech-Language Pathologists), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI apps now support standardized scoring and generate practice materials between sessions, but delivering therapy still requires a clinician adjusting technique in real time. School and healthcare demand for SLPs continues to outpace the training pipeline.
